characters.name: Abbey Whitman
characters.description: Abbey WhitmanJane the VirgincharacterBIOBornAbigail WhitmanAKAAbbeyResidenceMiamiEyesHairBrownBrownWORKOccupationOwner at Whitman Greeting Card CompanyRELATIONSHIPSRomance(s)Rafael Solano(ex-boyfriend)BACKSTAGEPortrayed byMinka KellySeason(s)Season 3First seenLast seenChapter Fifty-FiveChapter Fifty-SevenEpisodes3Abbey Whitmanis a character inSeason 3ofJane the Virgin. She is portrayed by Minka Kelly. Abbey andRafael Solanomeet and start dating two years after Rafael goes to prison. Abbey appears to be a calm, nice person and owns her own greeting card company. She and Rafael are together for over a year where they have never had a fight. Their relationship seems pretty smooth sailing and they're both comfortable in it, with Abbey having and is spending time with all 3 of Rafael's kids, and knows bothJaneandPetra. Abbey seems fine with both Jane and Petra being close with Rafael and a huge part of his life, and gets along great with Jane and vice versa. Things start to get complicated once Abbey starts talking about them moving in together and their future, which Rafael is hesitant to deal with. Rafael realizes he's not in love with Abbey, and unfortunately she overhears his conversation with Petra, where Rafael asks Petra for advice. Rafael ends things with Abbey as they're not on the same page and, thinking Petra is at fault, Abbey stealsScott Archuletta's book containing a mess of secrets about the Solanos,The Marbellaand Petra over the years and gives it to theMiami Police Department. characters.name: Adam Alvaro
characters.description: Adam AlvaroJane the VirgincharacterBIOBornAdam Eduardo AlvaroBirthdayAugust 23 – September 22[1]AKABabe-Adam(byXo)ResidenceLos Angeles, CaliforniaFORMERLY:Miami, FloridaFort Greene, NYEyesHairBrownDark BrownWORKOccupationComic Book ArtistRELATIONSHIPSMarital StatusSingleRomance(s)Jane Villanueva(ex-girlfriend/ex-fiancée)Heather(ex-girlfriend)Alex Taylor(ex-boyfriend)FamilyMs. Alvaro †(sister)BACKSTAGEPortrayed byTyler PoseySeason(s)3•4First seenLast seenChapter Sixty-FourChapter SeventyEpisodes7Adam Alvarois a recurring character onJane the Virgin. He is portrayed byTyler Posey. Adam is a comic book artist in his late 20s, living in a loft in Miami. He is a romantic at heart and finding Michael's letter led Adam to break up with his then-girlfriend Heather (even though he had had her name tattooed on his arm — it now says 'heathen'). Adam also carried Michael's letter around in his wallet after finding it in 2017. Sadly, his sister passed away from cancer. He loves drawing, has an interest in astrology and plays one song on the guitar — the theme that plays whenever Jane experiences romantic moments, which is also his own love theme. He believes in signs. He studied art at NYU. 2010 Adam is a comic book artist in his late 20s, living in a loft in Miami. She is portrayed byIvonne Coll. Alba is the mother ofXiomara Villanuevaand the grandmother ofJane. Alba loves telenovelas, her favorite beingThe Passions of Santos. Alba is a very big believer in God and a devout Catholic. She believes that God has our lives in his hands and will guide us. She usually speaks Spanish, only speaking English if needed. However, she can understand English perfectly. Alba is really the Villanueva rock throughout any tumultuous relationships and events that may occur in her daughter and granddaughter's lives. During any tension between Xo and Jane, Alba will provide clarity and often a bit of grounded, tongue-in-cheek advice or a remark to lighten existence. characters.name: Alex
characters.description: AlexJane the VirgincharacterBIOBornAlexander (last name n/a)EyesHairBrownDark BrownWORKOccupationChild School AideBACKSTAGEPortrayed byDeniz AkdenizSeason(s)Season 3First seenLast seenChapter Fifty-EightChapter Fifty-NineEpisodes2Alexis a character inSeason 3ofJane the Virgin. He is portrayed by Deniz Akdeniz. Alex is a kind guy and is appropriately great with kids since his job is being a child aide at a school. At first, Jane is hesitant to hire a male aide because she has a bias towards females, but Jane eventually hires him when the female aide she initially chose turns out to be a huge gossip. He is great with Mateo and begins to help Mateo fix his impulse issues. During a meeting with Alex, Jane opens up about Michael's death without crying. Alex was also surprised that Mateo was not affected by Michael's death and/or Rafael's brief imprisonment. After this meeting with Jane and Rafael, Alex is presumably considered friends with them. Moreover, Jane thought he was being flirty at one point with the coy smiles, eye contact, and touching, but Jane realizes that Alex is clearly not interested when he blatantly tells her; Alex is just friendly, not flirty.

Anežka first comes across as very animated, yet shy and seemingly rather jumpy and clumsy, her life in a Czech orphanage with a rat as her only friend had made her fearful and defensive, yet eager to please and enthusiastic in her exchanges with other people. characters.name: Bruce
characters.description: BruceJane the VirgincharacterBIOBornBruceBirthdayin his 40sResidenceformerlyBruce and Xo's HouseEyesHairBrownDark BrownWORKOccupationOwner & Attorney atGossard & EdwardsRELATIONSHIPSMarital StatusDivorcedRomance(s)Xiomara Villanueva(ex-fiancée)Unnamed woman(ex-wife)FamilyTess(daughter)BACKSTAGEPortrayed byRicardo ChaviraSeason(s)Season 3First seenLast seenChapter FiftyChapter Fifty-NineEpisodes8Bruceis a recurring character inSeason 3ofJane the Virgin. He is portrayed byRicardo Chavira. Bruce is a lawyer fromMiami, who started having an affair withXiomarawhile he was married. They seem to break if off onceRogeliocomes back into Xo's life, Bruce never seen at that time. When they meet again, he comes off as a good guy, relaxed, kind and helpful, and treats Xiomara well. They start off tense, as Xo was unhappy with the way things ended between them, with him ultimately never following through on leaving his wife for Xo. They did have real feelings for each other despite the circumstances, and when they reconnect 2 years later, Bruce has divorced. They soon find their old feelings rekindled and tentatively start dating.Janeis a hard sell on this, as is Bruce's 16-year-old daughter, hurt by her parents divorce and her father's adultery. Bruce and Xiomara work hard to make amends with the respective children and really want to be together. They are together for 3 years, during which they move in together and seem really settled and happy. This period, however, is also marked by the tension between Rogelio and Xiomara and Bruce is sometimes caught in the middle, although he of course supports Xiomara.[1]

characters.name: Darci Factor
characters.description: Darci FactorJane the VirgincharacterBIOBornDarci FactorFromNew York, NYResidenceNew YorkFORMERLY:Miami, FloridaEyesHairBrownLight/Sandy BrownWORKOccupationLove Dealer™Reality Star ofThe De La Vega-Factor FactorRELATIONSHIPSMarital StatusEngagedRomance(s)Esteban Santiago(fiancé)Rogelio De La Vega(ex-boyfriend/baby daddy)FamilyBaby De La Vega Factor(daughter)BACKSTAGEPortrayed byJustina MachadoSeason(s)3•4•5First seenLast seenChapter Fifty-OneChapter One-HundredEpisodes19Darci Factoris a recurring character onJane the Virgin. She is portrayed byJustina Machado. Darci is a straightforward, no-nonsense woman, who owns and runs a successful matchmaking company and takes on Rogelio as a client. While great at what she does, Darci expresses interest in having a child — with Rogelio, as a non-romantic co-parental unit. Darci is very set on what she wants in life, also in regards to having a child, as she finds love messy and has been hurt before, seeing her reluctant to open her heart again. Darci works as a successful matchmaker and seems to have dedicated herself completely to her work in her life. She is tenacious, talented and ambitious. After meeting Rogelio, who wants to have a child, Darci decides to enter into a non-romantic parenting agreement with him, as she, too, wants a child. This quickly falls through when Rogelio develops feelings for Darci and she asks him to get over it so they can move forward with their plans. He's unable to and convinces Darci that they should date. On their date, Darci is very critical and later reveals to Rogelio that she was hurt before and has a hard time opening herself up for fear of getting hurt, as well as the looming presence of Xiomara. Confiding this in Rogelio allows Darci to consider them earnestly. Simultaneously, Darci sells the concept of a matchmaker falling in love as a reality tv-series, which is greenlit. Rogelio is involved, but Darci wants him to be her co-star. After lashing out at Darci when his scene is cut from his big indie film, Rogelio apologises by agreeing to star in her reality show, entitledThe De La Vega-Factor Factor.
